content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
, (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
'I 
Jeffrey D. Gospe 
4595 Pearl Drive 
Santa Rosa, CA 95409 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-207 
Dear Mr. Gospe: 
January 5, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section l 090. 
QUESTION 
As a trustee of the Board of Education for the Rincon Valley Union School District 
("District") may you participate in a decision involving the District purchasing real property that is 
located within 1,250 feet of your home residence? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. You may participate in a District decision to purchase real property located near your 
home because there will be no reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your real property 
interest. 
FACTS 
You are serving as a trustee of the Board of Education for the District in Santa Rosa, 
California. In May 2016, your school board entered into a purchase option agreement for real 
property consisting of five contiguous parcels for a total of 15 acres. For more than 50 years, that 
property served as the private Oak Park Swim & Tennis Club, but is now vacant. The properties are 
being considered for purchase as part of the District's long term plans to possibly construct another 
school campus and/or other District facilities in several years. There are few large, undeveloped 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-207 
Page No. 2 
parcels ofland remaining within the boundaries of the District. At the present time the future 
intended use for the property has not been fully determined. 
The property was appraised at $3 .1 million in May 2016, and the option agreement provides 
ample time for the District to go through the CEQA and public review process. The purchase is 
contingent on the public review process, compliance with CEQA, and the District not paying above 
fair market value for the property. 
You live in a single-family home that is approximately 1,250 feet from the closest of the 
five parcels being considered for purchase. You cannot see the properties from your home, as they 
are separated by several streets, many homes, a creek, and a middle school of a different school 
district. 
Other than the probable impact of increased traffic on a neighborhood street (that already 
has two schools located on it) if and when a new school might be constructed at this location many 
years in the future, you do not see any positive or negative impacts to your property value. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise 
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a 
financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a decision, 
within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect on one or more of the official's interests. Section 87103 also identifies the financial 
interests that may give rise to a conflict of interest under the Act. Of those interests, the facts 
presented and the decision at issue implicate the following: 
• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or 
more. (Section 87103(b); Regulation 18702.2.) 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
For a financial interest not "explicitly involved" in the decision at issue, the financial effect 
of the decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701 (b )). Regulation 18702.2 
provides the relevant materiality standards for financial interests in real property. None of the 
criteria provided in this regulation are met under the facts you have provided. 
Additionally, Regulation 18702 provides that "the financial effect of a governmental 
decision is not material ifit is nominal, inconsequential, or insignificant." The District's decision to 
purchase real property would have only a nominal, inconsequential or insignificant effect upon your 
real property that is located 1,250 feet away and separated from the subject property to be 
purchased by several streets, many homes, a creek, and a middle school of a different school 
district. Any financial effect upon your real property as a result of increased traffic that you have 
described would occur only after future governmental decisions are made about what is to be built 
on the property. Therefore, there will be no reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon 
your financial interest as a result of the current decision before the District. 


File No. A-16-207 
Page No. 3 
Since there is no reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your financial interest, 
you do not have a conflict of interest in the current decision. However, please keep in mind that 
future governmental decisions regarding the property to be purchased by the District, such as those 
involving what is to be constructed on the property, could have a financial effect upon your 
property and you should consider seeking further assistance at that time. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
SKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
?t 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

As a trustee of the Board of Education for the Rincon Valley Union School District 
("District") may you participate in a decision involving the District purchasing real property that is 
located within 1,250 feet of your home residence?

Yes. You may participate in a District decision to purchase real property located near your 
home because there will be no reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your real property 
interest.

QUESTION: As a trustee of the Board of Education for the Rincon Valley Union School District 
("District") may you participate in a decision involving the District purchasing real property that is 
located within 1,250 feet of your home residence?

CONCLUSION: Yes. You may participate in a District decision to purchase real property located near your 
home because there will be no reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your real property 
interest.
